# Raw research: Pricing psychology & value models (Prompt 20)

**Date:** 2026-04-12  
**Prompt:** `docs/build/research/prompts/20-pricing-psychology-value-models.md`  
**Skills applied:** academic-research (conceptual synthesis), poll-opinion-research / public pricing pages & sector reports

---

## 1. Argument index trace (repo-facing)

| Claim / theme | ID in prompt | Notes for internal consistency |
|---------------|--------------|--------------------------------|
| Revolutionary pricing $1K + 10% | `C-SP05`, messaging | Treat as **positioning** vs. custom dev & publisher economics, not a peer-reviewed “revolution.” |
| AI cost curve enables underpricing | `articles-044` | Operational claim: tie to **marginal cost of software** + **automation of build**, not to leader outcomes. |
| $140K–$380K leader monetization | `business-042` | Per Prompt 03 raw trace: verify shard IDs before citing as “business-042”; use as **scenario band**, not median. |
| 90/10 aligned incentives | `C-BM01` | Classic **principal–agent** framing; literature supports alignment **when measurement is trusted** and **effort is observable-ish**. |
| Cap 100 leaders | `C-POS06` | Implies **minimum viable ARPU** on the 10% leg; sensitivity analysis below. |

---

## 2. Pricing psychology — bibliography (starter set)

### Behavioral economics & pricing

| Work / author | Relevance to Movemental questions |
|---------------|-----------------------------------|
| Kahneman & Tversky; Thaler — **mental accounting**, **endowment effect**, **transaction utility** | “10% of future revenue” is mentally booked differently than an equivalent monthly SaaS fee; loss aversion can spike if revenue feels “monitored.” |
| Ariely — **relativity**, **anchoring**, **decoy effects** (*Predictably Irrational*, related papers) | $1K + 10% vs. $100K build or 85–90% publisher framing is **deliberate anchor contrast**; risk is **too-good-to-be-true** if anchor feels cartoonish. |
| Gourville (1998) — “Pennies-a-day” framing | Reframing annual costs into smaller units increases uptake; inverse: **percent-of-revenue** can feel large if the base grows. |
| Hossain & Morgan (2006) & follow-on — **pay-what-you-want** / nonlinear pricing experiments | Shows **trust** and **reference prices** matter; extreme low fixed fees can **reduce perceived quality** unless quality signals elsewhere. |
| Simonson & Tversky — **context-dependent preferences** | Category: “ministry / movement leader” may tolerate revenue share **if** peers do and mission alignment is salient. |

### Professional services & value-based pricing

| Work / author | Relevance |
|---------------|-----------|
| Maister, Green & Galford — *The Trusted Advisor* | Trust-based selling: **fee structure clarity** and **conflict transparency** matter as much as number. |
| Weiss — value-based fees | Supports **taking % of value created** when scope is uncertain; requires **credible measurement** and **mutual definition of “revenue.”** |

### SaaS / PLG pricing (industry, not academic)

| Source type | Use |
|-------------|-----|
| OpenView, Paddle, ProfitWell (Patrick Campbell) archives / reports | Willingness-to-pay experiments, discounting harms, expansion revenue — useful for **“too cheap”** and packaging. |
| KeyBanc / SaaS surveys (annual) | Median growth, NRR — for **platform sustainability** narratives, not for ministry ethics. |

---

## 3. Competitor & comparable pricing — table (web-checked snapshot, 2026-04)

**Caveat:** Vendor prices change; faith products are often **quote-based**. Treat as **order-of-magnitude positioning**, not contract advice.

### Creator / course / community SaaS (B2C-leaning)

| Platform | Public list pricing (approx.) | Revenue / transaction take | Notes |
|----------|--------------------------------|------------------------------|--------|
| **Kajabi** | **$89–$399/mo** published tiers (Kickstarter through Pro); **Jan 2026** pricing update communicated for legacy migrations | **0%** platform fee on digital products (per vendor marketing) | Annual billing ~20% off common pattern. |
| **Teachable** | **$39–$399/mo** (Starter–Advanced post-2025 restructure) | **7.5%** on Starter; **0%** platform fee on Builder+ (payment processing still applies) | Starter’s % fee dominates TCO at low revenue. |
| **Mighty Networks** | **~$79 / $179 / $354/mo** + **Mighty Pro** custom (~**$33k+/yr** cited in secondary summaries) | **~2% → 0.5%** transaction fees on paid plans (tiered) | “Community + course” closer analog to “networked leader home.” |
| **Squarespace** | **~$16–$65/mo** (personal/business/commerce bands) | Commerce plans + payment processor fees | Weak functional analog for deep LMS + community, but **strong mental anchor** for “cheap website.” |

### Faith-adjacent / church stack (partial public pricing)

| Platform | What is public | Implication |
|----------|------------------|-------------|
| **Subsplash** | **Giving** at **0/mo** + **processing** (~**2.3% + $0.30** card typical); **One** = **custom / church-size** | Full stack comparable to “digital ministry OS” is **enterprise-style quoting**; third-party estimates often **$300–$500+/mo** for branded app bundles — **verify per RFP**. |
| **Faithlife Proclaim** | **$0–$79/mo** tiers for presentation product (not full Movemental analog) | Shows **freemium + low monthly** norm in ministry software; **different category** but shapes **price taste**. |
| **Planning Center** | Module-based; low per-module monthly for many churches | Reinforces **subscription comfort** and **modular** budgeting in churches. |

### Custom build / agency (order of magnitude)

| Channel | Typical range (USD, 2024–2026 market chatter) | Notes |
|---------|-----------------------------------------------|--------|
| **Custom WordPress + LMS + membership** | **$15k–$80k+** build + **$200–$2k/mo** care/hosting | Highly variable on integrations, SSO, mobile, compliance. |
| **Thought-leader / personal brand agency** | **$25k–$150k+** for serious site + brand + launch | Retainers **$3k–$15k/mo** common for ongoing growth teams. |

**Movemental contrast (internal model):** **$1,000** setup reads as **two orders of magnitude below** bespoke agency **and** **well below** first-year **Kajabi Pro + implementation**. **10%** reads as **between** Teachable Starter’s platform take **and** publisher royalty **framing**, but **only** if “revenue” is defined in ways leaders accept.

---

## 4. Unit economics — scenarios (illustrative, not a forecast)

**Assumptions from prompt:** 100 leaders cap; **$1,000** setup each; **10%** of “digital revenue” (define in contract).

### 4.1 Setup fee only

- 100 × **$1,000** = **$100,000** one-time.
- Insufficient alone to run a full platform org for long; clearly **a acquisition / alignment deposit**, not ARR.

### 4.2 Uniform distribution (prompt’s toy case)

- If each leader generates **$50k/yr** digital revenue → **$5k** × 100 = **$500k/yr** from 10% leg.
- **Combined first year (setup + year-1 rev share):** **$600k** gross before costs.
- Whether **$500k/yr** recurring is “enough” depends on **headcount, infra, support depth, AI inference budget, legal, sales** — not derivable from public data here. **Sensitivity** is the honest output.

### 4.3 Pareto (80/20 on the 10% leg)

Assume **same** **$5M** total digital revenue across the network (500 × 10k average).

| Scenario | Top 20 leaders’ share of total digital $ | 10% leg from top 20 | 10% leg from remaining 80 | Total 10% leg |
|----------|------------------------------------------|----------------------|----------------------------|-----------------|
| Mild Pareto | 60% | $300k | $200k | **$500k** |
| Strong Pareto | 80% | $400k | $100k | **$500k** |

**Key insight:** If **total** digital revenue is fixed, Pareto **redistributes** who pays but not the numerator. **Risk** is **concentration**: churn of a few whales collapses recurring. **Mitigation** in product/GTM: **services depth** for high earners, **community value** for long tail so cap strategy does not **only** chase monetization maxers.

### 4.4 “AI cost curve” — defensible meaning

- **Development:** Copilot-class tooling lowers **time-to-CRUD** and **time-to-component**; does not remove **security, product judgment, support, compliance**.  
- **Operations:** Automated testing, monitoring, docs generation shrink **run cost per feature**.  
- **Does not guarantee** lower **customer acquisition cost** or **higher willingness-to-pay** on the leader side.

---

## 5. Nonprofit / ministry willingness-to-pay (public signals)

| Source | Finding (high level) |
|--------|----------------------|
| **NTEN — 2024 Nonprofit Digital Investments Report** | Emphasis on **how** budgets are spent, not raw “SaaS is evil”; **many orgs feel under-invested** in tech; **budget** is top barrier. |
| **NTEN blog / donor tech funding** | **~45%** feel they don’t spend enough on tech; **~77%** cite **lack of budget** (secondary reporting on NTEN materials — **pull PDF for exact wording** before external marketing use). |
| **TechSoup** (historical brand) | Strong **discount / donated license** expectation in nonprofit subculture — may **anchor** leaders to **low marginal software prices** unless value story is sharp. |

**Implication:** A **$1,000** entry can be **psychologically heavy** for small nonprofits **unless** paired with **grant-eligible framing**, **payment plans**, or **clear ROI** (time saved, donations captured, compliance). For **individual movement leaders** with **speaking / book / course income**, **$1,000** is often **smaller than one keynote fee** — very different segments.

---

## 6. Ethical / theological notes (non-exhaustive)

- **1 Timothy 5:18** / **Luke 10:7** — “The laborer deserves his wages”: classical support for **paying workers and vendors**; does not settle **optimal** pricing model.  
- **Thomas Aquinas on just price** (tradition summary): price should reflect **common estimation** in the community — supports **transparency** and **non-deception** more than a specific %.  
- **Perverse incentive risk (Prompt Q16):** Revenue share can **nudge** toward **monetization maximalism**. Mitigations: **explicit mission charter**, **non-monetary success metrics** in coaching, **opt-out / cap** structures for certain revenue types, **scholarship fund** for low-revenue leaders.

---

## 7. Open questions / next primary research

1. **Define “revenue”** for the 10%: donations vs. course sales vs. speaking — each has different **psychological** and **audit** load.  
2. **Run 5–8 semi-structured interviews** with target leaders on **% vs. flat fee** (qualitative beats guessing anxiety).  
3. **Refresh** Subsplash / Pushpay / Ministry Grid **list + RFP quotes** annually — table goes stale fast.  
4. **Model AI variable cost** per active leader (support + inference) to pair with revenue scenarios.
